chunk_summary.idea_trajectory_justification: The group is exploring the fundamental cost drivers and technical limitations of CO2 electrolysis, asking questions to map the problem space rather than converging on a specific project.
chunk_summary.collective_engagement_justification: Non-speaking participants like Venkat and Jenny show visible head nods and active listening postures, and multiple participants jump in with relevant questions.
chunk_summary.cross_disciplinary_bridging_description: Translating chemical engineering TEA metrics into fundamental chemistry terms.
chunk_summary.problem_specificity_justification: The group deeply explores the technical bottlenecks of CO2 reduction (membrane costs, single-pass conversion) but has not yet formulated a specific joint research question.
chunk_summary.decision_crystallization_justification: The discussion is focused on knowledge sharing and understanding the state of the art rather than proposing a specific project.
chunk_summary.funding_reference_description: Venkat mentions ARPA-E IONICS target costs for membranes.
chunk_summary.notable_observation: The group effectively uses cross-disciplinary questioning (e.g., Jordi asking to translate engineering metrics to chemistry terms) to build a shared understanding of the fundamental bottlenecks in CO2 electrolysis.

ideas_currently_on_table: Reactor engineering to solve single-pass conversion limits
CO electrolysis as a workaround for CO2 reduction bottlenecks
chunk_summaries: The group engaged in a deep, divergent exploration of the fundamental cost drivers and technical bottlenecks in CO2 electrolysis, heavily relying on Wilson Smith's expertise in techno-economic assessments. Jordi Cabana and Jenny Yang actively asked cross-disciplinary questions to translate engineering metrics into chemistry terms, revealing that single-pass conversion efficiency and membrane costs are major hurdles. The chunk ended with the group understanding that direct CO electrolysis might be a more viable industrial pathway than direct CO2 reduction, though no specific joint project was proposed.
